15:31.quote: It was after half past three when the hotel manager came to see Hetty. She had made several applications to the front desk since Sean O'Donnell had left, but to no avail.
15:31.title: New York
15:31.author: Edward Rutherfurd

15:32.quote: At 3:32 precisely, I noticed Kaitlyn striding confidently past the Wok House. She saw me the moment I raised my hand, flashed her very white and newly straightened teeth at me, and headed over.
15:32.title: The Fault in Our Stars
15:32.author: John Green

15:33.quote: I picked up my briefcase, glancing at my watch again as I did so. Three thirty-three.
15:33.author: Stephen King

15:34.quote: It was 15:34 now and the sleepiness felt like lead inside him.
15:34.title: Devil's Peak
15:34.author: Deon Meyer

15:35.quote: By three-thirty-five business really winds down. I have already sold my ladderback chairs and my Scottish cardigans. I'm not even sure now why I've sold all these things, except perhaps so as not to be left out of this giant insult to one's life that is a yard sale, this general project of getting rid quick.
15:35.title: Anagrams
15:35.author: Lorrie Moore

15:36.quote: Meanwhile a girl was standing in an airport concourse. A big clock suspended from the ceiling said it was twenty-four minutes to four.
15:36.title: The Intruders
15:36.author: Michael Marshall

15:37.quote: The explosion was now officially designated an 'Act of God'. But, thought Dirk, what god? And why? What god would be hanging around Terminal Two of Heathrow Airport trying to catch the 15.37 flight to Oslo?
15:37.title: The Long Dark Tea Time of the Soul
15:37.author: Douglas Adams

15:38.quote: Mendez took a marker and added to the line for the day Karly Vickers disappeared: 15:38 traffic ticket issued by F. Farman. He added to the line for Thursday: L. Warwick index finger in possession of D. Farman. Leone came over, tapped a finger on the line about the traffic citation, and raised his eyebrows.
15:38.title: Deeper than the Dead
15:38.author: Tami Hoag

15:39.quote: I lived two lives in late 1965 and early 1963, one in Dallas and one in Jodie. They came together at three thirty-nine in the afternoon of April 10.
15:39.author: Stephen King

15:40.quote: Why had nobody else arrived? Could he be too early? But no! They had said half past three and it was at least twenty to four. He hastened towards the gate.
15:40.title: Keep the Aspidistra Flying
15:40.author: George Orwell

15:40.quote: 'Light hair, red-checked gingham dress, and straw hat' - all she knows, indeed! Well, I'd be ashamed ter own it up, that I would, I would - and her my onliest niece what was a-comin' from 'way across the continent!' Promptly at twenty minutes to four the next afternoon Timothy and Nancy drove off in the open buggy to meet the expected guest.
15:40.title: Pollyanna
15:40.author: Eleanor H. Porter

15:41.quote: At 15:41 GMT, the Cessna's engine began to cut out and the plane - presumably out of fuel - began to lose altitude
15:41.title: The Crow Road
15:41.author: Iain Banks

15:41.quote: It's nineteen minutes to four. Mum and Dad are looking very stressed. Dad's put his elbow in his beer three times. A couple of secs ago a thought hit me. Perhaps they're worried that when the kids arrive, they might all try to bash me up.
15:41.title: Belly Flop
15:41.author: Morris Gleitzman

15:42.quote: So that, who knows? At that sudden distance I might not have even stopped, being already in motion again, raising my hand and arm in return and on, across the alley, striding on as you would naturally expect a county attorney to be striding along a side street at forty-two minutes past three in the afternoon; one whole block more for safety and then safe, inviolate still the intactness, unthreatened again.
15:42.title: The Town
15:42.author: William Faulkner

15:43.quote: He reached out a hand toward her, but she gave him such a terrible look that he pulled back and let her alone. At three forty-three, she set her jaw and kept going.
15:43.title: Doll Bones
15:43.author: Holly Black

15:44.quote: The armed response team hastily assembled from Strängnäs arrived at Bjurman's summer cabin at 3.44 p.m.
15:44.title: The Girl Who Played With Fire
15:44.author: Stieg Larsson

15:45.quote: I opened my notebook, flipped almost to the end before I found a blank page, and wrote 'October 5th, 3.45pm, Dunning to Longview Cem, puts flowers on parents' (?) graves. Rain.' I had what I wanted.
15:45.author: Stephen King

15:46.quote: I authorize you to leave this room at fourteen minutes to four.... Not a moment before fourteen minutes to four.... Is it understood?'
15:46.title: The Blonde Lady
15:46.author: Maurice Leblanc

15:47.quote: 'I said now, Mr. Maryk. Now, according to my watch, is not the morning, but forty-seven minutes past three. If you get no results by eight o'clock this morning I shall solve the mystery myself-noting duly for future fitness reports the failure of the board to carry out its assignment.'
15:47.title: The Caine Mutiny
15:47.author: Herman Wouk

15:48.quote: At 15:48 the satellite passed out of the 'area of interest' determined by the Madrid computer.
15:48.title: Doomsday Ultimatum
15:48.author: James Follett

15:49.quote: Anthony picked up the note and placed it beside his lordship. Then he left the room and returned to his work. Lord Pomfret was exceedingly wrath. In fact, he brooded over the incident. This augured ill for Anthony. The cold fact that in due season—to be precise, at eleven minutes to four that same afternoon—Slip Along won his race easily did not improve matters.
15:49.title: Anthony Lyveden
15:49.author: Dornford Yates

15:50.quote: When I arrived at the tearoom, I did a quick circuit, looking for the red hat. There were no women with red hats. My watch showed ten minutes to four.
15:50.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
15:50.author: Haruki Murakami

15:51.quote: The telegram seems to say, 'The Sovereigns and the Royal Children expect themselves at Rome tomorrow at fifty-one minutes after fifteen o'clock.'
15:51.title: Italian Without a Master
15:51.author: Mark Twain

15:52.quote: He came to a curve, and there was the lay-by, just where McCready had said it would be. He checked his watch; eight minutes to four. The road was empty.
15:52.title: The Deceiver
15:52.author: Frederick Forsyth

15:53.quote: It was like the clouds lifting away from the sun. Jodie glanced at Reacher. He glanced at the clock. Seven minutes to four. Less than three hours to go.
15:53.title: Tripwire
15:53.author: Lee Child

15:54.quote: She hurriedly fed the donkey and the remaining rabbits, and scrambled into her car at six minutes to four, the dog following her unenthusiastically. 'Your supper will have to wait until we get back,' Thea told her. 'It's too early to have it now.'
15:54.title: Fear in the Cotswolds
15:54.author: Rebecca Tope

15:55.quote: Blomkvist glanced at the clock. It was five minutes to four. 'Do you want to see her? She's still afraid that you won't want to after you found out what she did.'
15:55.title: The Girl With The Dragon Tattoo
15:55.author: Stieg Larsson

15:56.quote: At four minutes to four o'clock, Jacob presses blotting paper over the page on his desk in Warehouse Eik. He drinks another cup of water of which he shall sweat every last drop.
15:56.title: The Thousand Autumns of Jacob de Zoet
15:56.author: David Mitchell

15:57.quote: The clerk had stepped into the hall, and when he came leisurely in at three minutes to four, Caxton discovered that he had written his petition on the back of a blank marriage license.
15:57.title: The Colonel's Dream
15:57.author: Charles W. Chesnutt

15:58.quote: A hum of tolling bells makes itself heard, but not sharply. At three-fifty-eight a waiting interval. Presently a long procession of gentlemen in evening dress comes in sight and approaches until it is near to the square, then falls back against the wall of soldiers at the sidewalk, and the white shirt-fronts show like snowflakes and are very conspicuous where so much warm color is all about.
15:58.title: The Memorable Assassination
15:58.author: Mark Twain

15:59.quote: As it lacked one minute to four when Hedworth Westerling, chief of staff in name as well as power now, alighted from the gray automobile that turned in at the Galland drive, the chauffeur thought well enough of himself to forget the crush of supplies and ambulances that had delayed His Excellency's car for at least ninety seconds in the main street of the town.
15:59.title: The Last Shot
15:59.author: Frederick Palmer

16:00.quote: He played for twenty-two days, just as he said he would. Every day at four o'cock in the afternoon, regardless of how much fighting was going on around him.
16:00.title: The Cellist of Sarajevo
16:00.author: Steven Galloway

16:00.quote: I only found out much later that those flowers were called Four O'clock, and were not magic at all. The magic was in the seed, waiting to be watered and cared for, the real magic was life.
16:00.title: Ghost Generations
16:00.author: Susan May Gudge

16:00.quote: A little after four o'clock, Pippa meandered over to Dot's house carrying a bottle of wine she had been keeping in reserve and wondering if she could possibly be pregnant in spite of the vestigial coil still lodged in her uterus like astronaut litter abandoned on the moon.
16:00.title: The Private Lives of Pippa Lee
16:00.author: Rebecca Miller

16:00.quote: In the end, it was the Sunday afternoons he couldn't cope with, and that terrible listlessness that starts to set in about 2.55, when you know you've had all the baths you can usefully have that day, that however hard you stare at any given paragraph in the newspaper you will never actually read it, or use the revolutionary new pruning technique it describes, and that as you stare at the clock the hands will move relentlessly on to four o'clock, and you will enter the long dark teatime of the soul.
16:00.title: Life, the Universe and Everything
16:00.author: Douglas Adams

16:01.quote: Light is coming in through the curtains. Suddenly the digits on the clock radio look like a year. 1601. I woke up a bit early, don't have to be born for another 400 years.
16:01.title: 101 Reykjavik
16:01.author: Hallgrímur Helgason

16:02.quote: I'd just looked up at the clock, to make sure time wasn't getting away from me, when I heard the shot. It was two minutes after four. I didn't know what to do.
16:02.title: Southern Ghost
16:02.author: Carolyn G. Hart

16:03.quote: She read the page carefully and then said, '16.03 - cat goes to the toilet in front garden.'
16:03.title: What Was Lost
16:03.author: Catherine O'Flynn

16:04.quote: At four past four he was aware of a rustling train's rush down the steps, and now was like a man with his neck on the block, awaiting the axe.
16:04.title: The Lord of the Sea
16:04.author: M. P. Shiel

16:05.quote: I was standing at the top of the long flight, overlooking the red brick buildings that walled the snow-filled quad and preparing to catch the trolley back to the asylum, when a tall young man with a rather ugly and bespectacled, but intelligent face, came up and said, 'Could you please tell me the time?' I glanced at my watch. 'Five past four.'
16:05.title: The Bell Jar
16:05.author: Sylvia Plath

16:06.quote: At six minutes after four, Benny's Cadillac pulled up in front of Mr. Botelia's store, and Benny's mother stepped out of the car with Penelope, who was gnawing on the tip of an ice cream cone.
16:06.title: Follow Me
16:06.author: Joanna Scott

16:07.quote: But he released him immediately because the ladder slipped from under his feet and for an instant he was suspended in air and then he realised that he had died without Communion, without time to repent of anything or to say goodbye to anyone, at seven minutes after four on Pentecost Sunday.
16:07.title: Love in the Time of Cholera
16:07.author: Gabriel Garcia Marquez

16:08.quote: It was eight minutes after four. I still don't have a plan. Maybe the guys in the Nova, maybe they had a plan.
16:08.title: The Monkey's Raincoat
16:08.author: Robert Crais

16:09.quote:
I have to hang up now, Rosemary said. 'I just wanted to know if there was any improvement.'
'No, there isn't. It was nice of you to call.' She hung up. It was nine minutes after four.
16:09.title: Rosemary's Baby
16:09.author: Ira Levin

16:10.quote: He began speaking at ten past four. His voice was a little hoarse, and he said he had a sore throat. He paused, then said: 'I appear before the House of Commons today with the most terrible responsibility that can fall on the shoulders of any living man.'
16:10.title: Fall of Giants
16:10.author: Ken Follett

16:11.quote: 4:11 P.M. Thurs. A Huey helicopter flies east overhead as the last of the U.S. Marines make ready to leave the beach; a buzzard dangles in the thermals closer over the town.
16:11.title: Seek
16:11.author: Denis Johnson

16:12.quote: At precisely twelve minutes after four a body of cavalry rode into the square, four abreast, clearing a way for the funeral cortege.
16:12.title: The Empty Mirror
16:12.author: J. Sydney Jones

16:13.quote: But at precisely 4.13pm, the fifty thousand spectators saw the totally unexpected happen, before their very eyes. From the most crowded section of the southern grandstand, an apparition suddenly emerged.
16:13.title: Aunt Julia and the Scriptwriter
16:13.author: Mario Vargas Llosa

16:14.quote: Then at 4.14pm on March 12 I moved behind zinc-zirconium-not-to-be-revealed-compounds protecting me in this hill, and God have mercy but the struggle is just exchanged for the next one, which is exhausting me further as I say, to separate the true from the false.
16:14.title: Already Dead
16:14.author: Denis Johnson

16:15.quote: It is only a quarter past four, (shewing his watch) and you are not now in Bath. No theatre, no rooms to prepare for. Half an hour at Northanger must be enough.
16:15.title: Northanger Abbey
16:15.author: Jane Austen

16:15.quote: The sun had begun to sink in the west, and the shadow of an oak branch had crept across my knees. My watch said it was 4.15.
16:15.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
16:15.author: Haruki Murakami

16:16.quote: 4.16pm The terrace outside the bar is packed, and Igor feels proud of his ability to plan things, because even though he's never been to Cannes before, he had foreseen precisely this situation and reserved a table.
16:16.title: The Winner Stands Alone
16:16.author: Paulo Coelho

16:17.quote: In the next instant she was running toward her house, unmindful of the bags she had dropped, seeing only the police cars, knowing as she glanced down at her watch and saw that it was seventeen minutes after four, that for her time had stopped.
16:17.title: Life Penalty
16:17.author: Joy Fielding

16:18.quote: And then, over the loudspeaker, she heard an announcement: the 16.18 was due to arrive on platform two, calling at Plymouth, Liskeard, Par, St Austell, Truro, Redruth, St Erth and Penzance. For a moment she stood, at a loss, while passengers flowed past her in both directions.
16:18.title: The Moment You Were Gone
16:18.author: Nicci Gerrard

16:19.quote: Jessica [4:19 PM] Don't tease me like that. I haven't been to a play in years. Charles [4:19 PM] Then it'll be my treat. You and the hubby can have big fun on me.
16:19.title: The Other Woman
16:19.author: Eric Jerome Dickey

16:20.quote: At twenty minutes past four - or, to put it another, blunter way, an hour and twenty minutes past what seemed to be all reasonable hope - the unmarried bride, her head down, a parent stationed on either side of her, was helped out of the building...
16:20.title: Raise High the Roof Beam, Carpenters
16:20.author: J. D. Salinger

16:21.quote: 4.21pm As they started on, Doug picked up a twig and after rubbing it off, started to move one end of it inside his mouth. 'What are you doing?' Bob asked. 'Brushing my teeth, nature style,' Doug answered. Bob grunted, smiling slightly. 'I'll use my toothbrush,' he said.
16:21.title: Hunted Past Reason
16:21.author: Richard Matheson

16:22.quote: Monday, 4.22pm Washington, D.C. Paul Hood took his daily late-afternoon look at the list of names on his computer monitor.
16:22.title: Balance of Power: Op-Center 05
16:22.author: Tom Clancy, Steve Pieczenik & Jeff Rovin

16:23.quote: They were hurrying west, trying to reach the river before sunset. The warming-related 'adjustments' to Earth's orbit had shortened the winter days, so that now, in January, sunset was taking place at 4.23.
16:23.title: A Visit from the Goon Squad
16:23.author: Jennifer Egan

16:24.quote: Mike winked at Ashley and continued with the remaining greetings and hugs and handshakes. The time was 4:24. Six hours to go. The minutes seemed to just melt away.
16:24.title: Teardrop
16:24.author: Travis Thrasher

16:25.quote: Once I had established my right to exercise, one of the policemen would take me out of my cell. The first day he said, 'It is 25 to four now. You have an hour's exercise. You finish at 25 past four.'
16:25.title: Inside Apartheid's Prison
16:25.author: Raymond Suttner

16:26.quote: It seemed all wrong to have thought of such a thing. She thought, 'I don't know him. Nor does he know me. Nor ever shall we.' She put her bare hand in the sun, where the wind would weather it. It was twenty-six minutes after four.
16:26.title: Catch-As-Catch-Can
16:26.author: Charlotte Armstrong

16:27.quote: Still no sign of Juliet. Patricia gave a surreptitious glance at her watch; twenty-seven minutes past four –no! nearly twenty-eight past – Oh, hurry up, Juliet, hurry up, do!
16:27.title: Visitors for the Chalet School
16:27.author: Elinor Brent-Dyer

16:28.quote: Same day: 4.28pm- Right turn at the second bus stop after the gas station. I stopped the car at the first ward post office and inquired at the corner tobacconists. Mr. M's house was the one to the right of the post office, visible diagonally in front of me.
16:28.title: The Ruined Map
16:28.author: Kobo Abe

16:29.quote: He described the anxieties of the trip, the delay at the aerodrome, the audacities of the Parisian taxicab. 'I got there,' he finished, 'at twenty-nine past four.
16:29.title: Love in These Days
16:29.author: Alec Waugh

16:30.quote: At four-thirty that afternoon in late January when I stepped into the parlour with Boo, my dog, Hutch was in his favourite armchair, scowling at the television, which he had muted.
16:30.title: Odd Hours
16:30.author: Dean Koontz

16:30.quote: I leave the office at four thirty, head up to Xclusive where I work out on free weights for an hour, then taxi across the park to Gio's in the Pierre Room for a facial, a manicure and, if time permits, a pedicure.
16:30.title: American Psycho
16:30.author: Bret Easton Ellis

16:31.quote: No sooner had he pocketed it again than a terrible suspicion came over him. With trembling fingers he accessed the list of outgoing calls. The most recent entry was his own home number, dialled at 16.31 on 16.07.
16:31.title: Night Work
16:31.author: Thomas Glavinic

16:32.quote: At exactly 16:32 hours of a bright spring afternoon, an Iricoil tanker proceeding down the St. Lawrence Seaway fouled its propellor on a tangle of steel cables, origin unknown.
16:32.title: To Ride Pegasus
16:32.author: Anne McCaffrey

16:33.quote: At 4.33pm, a short bald man puffing on a cigar arrived at the library. He approached a huge cabinet storing thousands of alphabetically arranged cards and slid a drawer out. The tips of his fingers were bandaged.
16:33.title: Havana World Series
16:33.author: José Latour

16:34.quote: A bedroom stocked with all the ordinary, usual things. There was a wardrobe in the corner. A bedside table with a collection of water glasses of varying ages and an alarm clock with red digital numbers- 4.34 p.m.
16:34.title: The Raw Shark Texts
16:34.author: Steven Hall

16:35.quote: It was thirty-five minutes past four. To expunge the sin of afternoon indolence, his first lapse since childhood, he washed his face in cold water three times, slapped his cheeks, and decided to walk all the way to the train station and back.
16:35.title: Last Man in Tower
16:35.author: Aravind Adiga

16:35.quote: The Voice shut itself off with a click, and then reopened conversation by announcing the arrival at Platform 9 of the 4.35 from Birmingham and Wolverhampton.
16:35.title: 4.50 from Paddington
16:35.author: Agatha Christie

16:36.quote: They watched their friend through the glass cover; the heaving of the chest became less and less, the breathing lower and lower, while a purple hue settled upon his body. At thirty-six minutes past four, the last division of the wheel had been reached.
16:36.title: A. D. 2000
16:36.author: Alvarado M. Fuller

16:37.quote: She should have been home by now. 1637. Yes. It's as if I had the date of a year on my arm. Every day is a piece of world history.
16:37.title: 101 Reykjavik
16:37.author: Hallgrímur Helgason

16:38.quote: I let him put me in the car and drive me to the station. At twenty-two minutes to five by the clock in the station house I was in front of a cell in the Mill River jail.
16:38.title: A Catskill Eagle
16:38.author: Robert B. Parker

16:39.quote: Harlem enjoys lazy Sabbath mornings, although the pace picks up again in the afternoon, after church. My watch read 4:39 p.m., and I realized that I hadn't eaten all day. I bought two slices of pizza from a sidewalk vendor on 122nd and Lenox Avenue and washed it down with a grape Snapple.
16:39.title: Blood Red Blues
16:39.author: Teddy Hayes

16:40.quote: Four forty P.M. Besta sang another hymn. Everyone knew something was wrong. How long did they wait? The mayor was going crazy inside, as was the mayor's wife, as was their daughter. Seiji could barely contain his rage. He was turning as red as his red tuxedo.
16:40.title: Trouble & Triumph
16:40.author: Tip 'T.I.' Harris with David Ritz

16:41.quote: She'd opened the door at nineteen minutes to five, thinking she had some measure of control over her world…. Now she knew it for the lie it was. Everything she once thought she knew was upside-down; everything had changed.
16:41.title: Who Do You Trust
16:41.author: Melissa James

16:42.quote: I'm always happy when I reach the finish line of a long-distance race, but this time it really struck me hard. I pumped my right fist into the air. The time was 4:42pm. Eleven hours and forty-two minutes since the start of the race.
16:42.title: What I Talk About When I Talk About Running
16:42.author: Haruki Murakami

16:43.quote: It seems as if the Indian mail train that leaves Bombay at 16:43 is the train I'm destined to be on. Destiny is a word with thousands of new connotations in India.
16:43.title: Indian Takeaway
16:43.author: Hardeep Singh Kohli

16:44.quote: At sixteen minutes to five Mr Beveridge stood by the side of the Clankwood Avenue, comfortably wrapped in Dr Escort's fur coat, and smoking with the greatest relish one of Dr Escott's undeniable cigars. It was almost dark, the air bit keen, the dim park with its population of black trees was filled with a frosty, eager stillness.
16:44.title: The Lunatic at Large
16:44.author: J. Storer Clouston

16:45.quote: At four-forty-five Miss Haddon went to tea with the Principal, who explained why she desired all the pupils to learn the same duet. It was part of her new co-ordinative system.
16:45.title: Co-ordination
16:45.author: E.M. Forster

16:46.quote: At 4:46 an obese, middle-aged man shuffled in. Wearing a starched guayabera and dark green pants, Ureña asked for a book on confectionery, then took a seat at the end of the same reading room. Evelina and Leticia exchanged astonished glances. It definitely was one of those days.
16:46.title: Havana World Series
16:46.author: José Latour

16:47.quote: But maybe it was more than that, maybe Affenlight had erred badly somehow, because here it was 4:49 by his watch, 4:47 by the wall clock, and Owen had not yet come.
16:47.title: The Art of Fielding
16:47.author: Chad Harbach

16:48.quote: By four forty-eight the five-man team had her back again and the urgent struggle to save Ellen's life continued. She'd now been undergoing surgery for the best part of two hours, and it was doubtful her body could sustain much more trauma.
16:48.title: Taking Chances
16:48.author: Susan Lewis

16:49.quote: 4:49 p.m., a bald-headed man wearing khakis and ankle-high deck shoes came out through the front door of the purple house on 21st Avenue East. The detectives had nicknamed him the General.
16:49.title: Beyond Recognition
16:49.author: Ridley Pearson

16:50.quote: But at ten minutes to five a girl ran up the steps of Number Eighteen and rang the bell. She was a girl of medium height, very straight and slim; and her fair hair, her cheerful smile, and the boyish suppleness of her body all contributed to a general effect of valiant gaiety, a sort of golden sunniness
16:50.title: Leave it to Psmith
16:50.author: P. G. Wodehouse

16:51.quote: Nine minutes to five. If this wasn't some new ordeal, intended to fray her nerves to shreds, if this important person really did exist, if he'd actually set up this appointment, and if, moreover, he arrived on time, then there were nine minutes left.
16:51.title: Compulsory Happiness
16:51.author: Norman Manea

16:52.quote: The corrida was to begin at five o'clock. The five-footed beasts make a point of arriving at the latest at eight or seven minutes to: ritual again. At eight minutes to five, there they were. The urchins gave them a tap on the shoulder: another bit of ritual.
16:52.title: Chaos and Night
16:52.author: Henry De Montherlant

16:53.quote: It was so quiet in the post office that Trinidad could hear the soft tick of the clock's second hand every time it moved. It was now seven minutes before five.
16:53.title: The Silence of Bonaventure Arrow
16:53.author: Rita Leganski

16:54.quote: At six minutes before five o'clock, Daisy Robinson, about to reach her own apartment door, paused to look and to listen. Something was out of order. Tess Rogan's door was standing wide open and, from within, Daisy could hear something being broken.
16:54.title: The Seventeen Widows of Sans Souci
16:54.author: Charlotte Armstrong

16:55.quote: About five minutes to five, just as they were all putting their things away for the night, Nimrod suddenly appeared in the house. He had come hoping to find some of them ready dressed to go home before the proper time.
16:55.title: The Ragged Trousered Philanthropists
16:55.author: Robert Tressell

16:56.quote: And when that final Friday came, when my packing was mostly done, she sat with my dad and me on the living-room couch at 4:56 P.M. and patiently awaited the arrival of the Good-bye to Miles Cavalry.
16:56.title: Looking for Alaska
16:56.author: John Green

16:57.quote: Then at three minutes to five — Pendel had somehow never doubted that Osnard would be punctual — along comes a brown Ford hatchback with an Avis sticker on the back window and pulls into the space reserved for customers.
16:57.title: The Tailor of Panama
16:57.author: John le Carré

16:58.quote: I was told that in his vest pocket he kept a chronometer instead of a watch. If someone asked him what time it was, he would say, 'A minute and twenty-one seconds to five.'
16:58.title: Vanvild Kava
16:58.author: Isaac Bashevis Singer

16:59.quote: At the end of that day's work Mma Ramotswe so engineered matters that she was standing at the door of her tiny white van at precisely the time – one minute to five – that the two apprentices came out of the garage entrance, wiping their greasy hands on a handful of the loose white lint provided by Mr J. L. B. Matekoni.
16:59.title: The Full Cupboard Of Life
16:59.author: Alexander McCall Smith

16:59.quote:
I do not wish, sir, said Julien to him gravely, 'to be alone for a single instant. Deign to observe,' he added, showing him the clock over their heads, 'that I have arrived at one minute to five.'
'So those little rascals at the seminary frightened you. It is very good of you to think of them,' said the abbé.
16:59.title: The Red and the Black
16:59.author: Stendhal

17:00.quote: At five o'clock adieux were waved, and the ponderous liner edged away from the long pier, slowly turned its nose seaward, discarded its tug, and headed for the widening water spaces that led to old world wonders.
17:00.title: The Horror at Red Hook
17:00.author: H. P. Lovecraft

17:00.quote: The rain stopped around 5 p.m. and a few of those people who were out and about expressed mild surprise when the rainbow failed to fade.
17:00.title: The Rainbow
17:00.author: Nicholas Royle

17:00.quote: The rest was death and death alone at five in the afternoon. [Lo demas era muerte y solo muerte a las cinco de la tarde]
17:00.title: Lament for Ignacio Sanchez Mejias
17:00.author: Federico García Lorca

17:00.quote: We motored, I remember, leaving London in the morning in a heavy shower of rain, coming to Manderley about five o'clock, in time for tea. I can see myself now, unsuitably dressed as usual, although a bride of seven weeks, in a tan-coloured stockinette frock, a small fur known as a stone marten round my neck, and over all a shapeless mackintosh, far too big for me and dragging to my ankles.
17:00.title: Rebecca
17:00.author: Daphne du Maurier

17:00.quote: Beyond his limits it is not wise to stray, lest we arrive at the land of gilded puppets, where Disraeli's dukes and duchesses feast forever on ortolans, and pompetones of larks, and lobster sandwiches; where young spendthrifts breakfast at five o'clock in the afternoon on soup and claret;
17:00.title: At the novelist's table
17:00.author: Agnes Repplier

17:01.quote: One minute after five. The seated guests were told that the ceremony would begin shortly. A little more patience was required.
17:01.title: Trouble & Triumph
17:01.author: Tip 'T.I.' Harris with David Ritz

17:02.quote: She stood up, shook her hair into place, smoothed her skirt and turned on the light. It was two minutes past five. She would have thought it midnight or five in the morning.
17:02.title: Duplicate Keys
17:02.author: Jane Smiley

17:03.quote:
'Good evening, Mrs. Scheindlin,' the man said before departing. 'Good evening, Chris. Say hello to the wife for me.'
'I sure will. Thanks. Bye,' he said, waving to Elliot, who returned the goodbye. It was 5:03 when Elliot rested the handset in its cradle.
17:03.title: Comrades in Miami
17:03.author: José Latour

17:04.quote: Frank Wamsley spotted his cousin Barbara and her husband and waved to them. Just ahead, he saw Marvin and his two friends. Suddenly the whole bridge convulsed. The time was 5:04 P.M. Steel screamed.
17:04.title: The Mothman Prophecies
17:04.author: John A. Keel

17:05.quote: That evening, Fanwell left the garage at his usual time, which was five minutes past five. Work ended officially at five o'clock, and Fanwell, being conscientious, insisted on working until the very last moment
17:05.title: The Limpopo Academy Of Private Detection
17:05.author: Alexander McCall Smith

17:06.quote: In her ledger the historic moment of delivery was later put at six past five, which was a lie but gave it force.
17:06.title: The Honourable Schoolboy
17:06.author: John le Carré

17:07.quote: Be ready to meet it. 'If you're turned t'other way you c'n git a quicker start if you want to run,' suggested Jim Borum, bracing himself with a fresh chew of tobacco. 'What time is it?' asked Wicker Bonner. Anderson Crow squinted up through the leafless treetops toward the setting sun; then he looked at the shadow of a sapling down on the bank. 'It's about seven minutes past five - in the evenin',' he said conclusively.
17:07.title: The Daughter of Anderson Crow
17:07.author: George Barr McCutcheon

17:07.quote: As I looked at its huge dial, and at the pendulum moving to and fro regularly and slowly in Space, as if its swinging meant to divide eternity, I saw its needles pointing to seven minutes past five.
17:07.title: Nightmare Tales
17:07.author: H. P. Blavatsky

17:08.quote: This, then, was the position in which my distant cousin Montrose found himself at eight minutes past five on this misty evening. A position calculated to test the fortitude of the sternest.
17:08.title: Blandings Castle and Elsewhere
17:08.author: P. G. Wodehouse

17:09.quote: She looked at her watch and saw it was nine minutes past five. It's happening, Daddy! The sun's going out! Yes, he agreed.
17:09.title: Gerald's Game
17:09.author: Stephen King

17:10.quote: Five ten P.M. A ground-to-ground cruise missile, launched from a tractor installed in the backyard of Leonard Sudavico's former home by Rashan and a crew of technicians from Afghanistan, exploded onto the Paul Clay estate in the exact spot where the life-size mermaid had once swum in the waterfall.
17:10.title: Trouble & Triumph
17:10.author: Tip 'T.I.' Harris with David Ritz

17:11.quote: At eleven minutes past five Dick Bellamy stopped Melchard's car outside the booking-office of somnolent Harthborough's dead-alive station - the junction of the single-line track to Whitebay and its bathing machines with the double-track branch of the G.N.R.
17:11.title: Ambrotox and Limping Dick
17:11.author: Oliver Fleming

17:12.quote: 'Well, here we are,' said Colonel Julyan, 'and it's exactly twelve minutes past five. We shall catch them in the middle of their tea. Better wait for a bit' Maxim lit a cigarette, and then stretched out his hand to me. He did not speak.
17:12.title: Rebecca
17:12.author: Daphne du Maurier

17:13.quote: So at 17.13 Callum Green was buying a necklace and something like a couple of hours later he was dead.
17:13.title: Lie in Wait
17:13.author: G. J. Minett

17:14.quote: 'Do you know what time it is, Atticus?' she said. 'Exactly fourteen minutes past five. The alarm clock's set for five-thirty. I want you to know that.'
17:14.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
17:14.author: Harper Lee

17:15.quote: When August Bach emerged from the gloomy chill of the air-conditioned Divisional Fighter Control bunker it was 17:15 hrs CET. The day had ripened into one of those mellow summer afternoons when the air is warm and sweet like soft toffee
17:15.title: Bomber
17:15.author: Len Deighton

17:16.quote: 'I think the first one I remember happening was on the London Underground, Circle line, at 17.16 p.m.' She went on to describe what had happened or not quite happened.
17:16.title: Money Never Sleeps
17:16.author: Stella Whitelaw

17:17.quote: And it was just seventeen minutes past five when Dick Ffrench, hanging in a frenzy of anxiety over the paddock fence circling the inside of the mile oval, uttered something resembling a howl and rushed to the gate to signal his recreant driver.
17:17.title: The Flying Mercury
17:17.author: Eleanor M. Ingram

17:18.quote: Lupin rose, without breaking his contemptuous silence, and took the sheet of paper. I remembered soon after that, at this moment, I happened to look at the clock. It was eighteen minutes past five.
17:18.title: The Confessions of Arsène Lupin
17:18.author: Maurice LeBlanc

17:19.quote: The call came at 5.19 p.m. The line was surprisingly clear. A man introduced himself as Major Liepa from the Riga police. Wallander made notes as he listened, occasionally answering a question.
17:19.title: The Dogs of Riga
17:19.author: Henning Mankell

17:20.quote: It was twenty past five and ten minutes to closing and she was running up her totals on the electronic till before watching Neighbours on TV with Marilyn her daughter who was upstairs minding her little girl.
17:20.title: The Night Manager
17:20.author: John le Carré

17:21.quote: He next proceeded to examine seriatim the dead man's pockets, turning each of them inside out and taking the contents, where they appeared adapted to his needs: for instance, a silken purse, through the interstices of which some gold was visible; a watch, which however had been injured by the explosion, and had stopt just at the moment - twenty-one minutes past five - when the catastrophe took place.
17:21.title: The Ancestral Footstep
17:21.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne

17:22.quote: Joubert drove there to help with the interrogation, compare alibis. Hours of listening to lies, sparring with teenage bravado and blatant provocation. But at 17:22 Lieutenant Petersen's patience eventually ran out.
17:22.title: Dead Before Dying
17:22.author: Deon Meyer

17:23.quote:
'I was wondering if we could meet for a drink.'
'What for?'
'Just for a chat. Do you know the Royal batsman, near Central Station? We could meet tomorrow at five?'
'Five twenty-three,' I said, to exert some control over the situation.
17:23.title: A Fraction of the Whole
17:23.author: Steve Toltz

17:24.quote: 'You know the game, rendezvous at...' and he looked up at the clock on the wall, '... exactly thirty hours from now at 17:24 hours and be ready to haul ass.'
17:24.title: End Run (Wing Commander)
17:24.author: William R. Forstchen & Christopher Stashef

17:25.quote: Now said Handsley, when Angela had poured out the last cup, 'it's twenty-five minutes past five, At half-past the Murder game is on'
17:25.title: A Man Lay Dead
17:25.author: Ngaio Marsh

17:26.quote:
There was a pause. 'Nick?'
'There's a train that leaves Totnes at twenty-six minutes past five this afternoon,' I said, 'and it reaches London at—'
17:26.title: Wildtrack
17:26.author: Bernard Cornwell

17:27.quote:
Ahem! Jerry teased. 'If you want to know the time, it is twenty-seven minutes past five. Your kindred spirit should appear in three minutes.'
'Here she comes now.' Marjorie had spied Katherine coming up the walk.
17:27.title: Marjorie Dean, College Sophomore
17:27.author: Josephine Chase

17:27.quote: At twenty-seven minutes past five, he rose and went indoors again; and until his death, which occurred in 1820, he never once failed to perform this incomprehensible pilgrimage.
17:27.title: The Confessions of Arsène Lupin
17:27.author: Maurice Leblanc

17:28.quote: And so, when he told her on May 15 at 5:28 in the afternoon that he had to be moving on, she passed out. She was in his bed at the time.
17:28.title: Smart Women
17:28.author: Judy Blume

17:29.quote: Vimes pulled out the Gooseberry and stared at it. At a time like this . . . 'Well?' he said. 'It's twenty-nine minutes past five, Insert Name Here,' said the imp nervously.
17:29.title: Thud!
17:29.author: Terry Pratchett

17:30.quote: It was half-past five before Holmes returned. He was bright, eager, and in excellent spirits, a mood which in his case alternated with fits of the blackest depression.
17:30.title: In Quest of a Solution
17:30.author: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

17:31.quote: They discussed the transmission: which of their friends, they wondered, was responsible for it? But at 17:31 Pick called back.
17:31.title: Heavy Water
17:31.author: Martin Amis

17:32.quote: It was exactly thirty-two minutes past five o'clock on the evening of Friday, December 9th, when the station-master at Anerley received the following communication by wire from the signal box at Forest Hill: 5.28 down from London Bridge just passed.
17:32.title: Cleek, the Master Detective
17:32.author: Thomas W. Hanshew

17:33.quote: At 5:33 p.m. there is a blast of two deep, resonant notes a major third apart. On another day there is the same blast at 12:54 p.m. On another, exactly 8 a.m.
17:33.title: Varieties of Disturbance
17:33.author: Lydia Davis

17:34.quote: I was as jittery as a virgin in a sorority house all day Thursday. By Friday I was certifiable. My phone rang at 5:34 in the afternoon. I had twenty-six minutes before my next client. It was Solberg.
17:34.title: One Hot Mess
17:34.author: Lois Greiman

17:35.quote: At twenty-five minutes to six he put the book back in the window-sill, dashed a few crumbs from his jacket, assumed a mortar-board cap that was lying on the tea-caddy, and went forth to his evening 'preparation duty.' The West Street was empty and shining golden with the sunset.
17:35.title: Love and Mr. Lewisham
17:35.author: H. G. Wells

17:36.quote:
'...at that moment I looked again at my watch because I wanted to be back at the Cathedral as Evensong ended at about twenty to six in order to talk to the choirmaster before he left. I saw I only had four minutes before it ended.'
'So that occurred at precisely twenty-four minutes before six?'
'Exactly.'
17:36.title: The Unburied
17:36.author: Charles Palliser

17:37.quote: Look, Lucille, said Joe when Lucille strolled into the office at 5:37. 'I don't know what you said to this gal, but it seems to have had exactly the opposite of the desired effect. She's got some bee in her bonnet about Harvard Law School.'
17:37.title: Lightning Rods
17:37.author: Helen DeWitt

17:38.quote: At 17.38 the city seemed deserted. It was the first Friday in the wake of the war. The men were still at the front. The women, the elderly and the children had retreated into their homes, trying to find the will to celebrate the Sabbath.
17:38.title: The Last Of Days
17:38.author: Moris Farhi

17:39.quote: It's 05:39 so we can have dinner, it's quick noodles. While they're in the hot water, Ma finds hard words to test me from the milk carton like nutritional that means food, and pasteurized that means laser guns zapped away the germs.
17:39.title: Room
17:39.author: Emma Donoghue

17:40.quote: Hey, young man, what time is it? 'What?' I said, is it 5:30 yet? 'Er, 5:40.' Heavens, they'll be starving. But then that's a good thing. Let them.'
17:40.title: I Love Dollars
17:40.author: Zhu Wen

17:41.quote: 'All right, people, let's haul ass. Sunset's at 17:41 and we've got twenty-three k to go.' Staff Sergeant Beyhn got to his feet and the platoon followed.
17:41.title: The Heart of Valor
17:41.author: Tanya Huff

17:42.quote: 'Well, they set sail, and after going ever so many thousand miles, or hundred - I forget which, but it don't matter - a great storm arose, a typhoon or simoon, perhaps both; and after slowly gathering up its energies for the space of twenty-nine days, seven hours, and twenty-three minutes, without counting the seconds, it burst upon them at exactly forty-two minutes past five, on the sixth day of the week.
17:42.title: The Dodge Club; Or, Italy in MDCCCLIX
17:42.author: James De Mille

17:43.quote: The first message came in at 5:43 in the afternoon, while I was at Vitality Yoga, attempting to prolong my vigor with mindful breath and deep core awareness, or at least that's what the brochure promised.
17:43.title: Elizabeth the First Wife
17:43.author: Lian Dolan

17:44.quote: Sometimes the heat of the fire, or the buzzing of the bluebottles on summer afternoons, would send her off into a doze, and at about a quarter to six she'd wake up with a tremendous start, glance at the clock on the mantelpiece, and then get into a stew because tea was going to be late.
17:44.title: Coming Up For Air
17:44.author: George Orwell

17:45.quote: Janice is not waiting for him in the lounge or beside the pool when at last around 5.45 they come home from playing the par-5 eighteenth. Instead one of the girls in their green and white uniforms comes over and tells him that his wife wants him to call home.
17:45.title: Rabbit Is Rich
17:45.author: John Updike

17:46.quote:
What time did you leave there? 'It must have been about quarter to six, for I was home at six.'
'And Miss Lindsay was there when you left.'
'Oh, yes, she was there when I left.' And then, Philip Barry's secret fear was confirmed.
17:46.title: The Luminous Face
17:46.author: Carolyn Wells

17:47.quote:
'Then I take it the matter is practically concluded,' said the clergyman. 'And that you very properly submit to return to your proper home. And now, if I may offer a suggestion, it is that we take tea. Freed of its tannin, nothing, I think, is more refreshing and stimulating.'
'There's a train from Lyndhurst at thirteen minutes to six,' said Widgery, unfolding a time table.
17:47.title: The Wheels of Chance: A Bicycling Idyll
17:47.author: H. G. Wells

17:48.quote: Father came home at 5:48 p.m. I heard him come through the front door. Then he came into the living room. He was wearing a lime green and sky blue check shirt and there was a double knot on one of his shoes but not on the other.
17:48.title: The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-time
17:48.author: Mark Haddon

17:49.quote:
'It's eleven minutes to six, Insert Name Here,' said the imp meekly. 'Good grief! Why didn't you tell me!'
'Because you said you didn't want to be interrupted!' the imp quavered.
17:49.title: Thud!
17:49.author: Terry Pratchett

17:50.quote:
'What time is it Jack?'
'Ten to six'
'Ten more minutes then.' I shuffle the cards. 'Time for a quick game of rummy?'
17:50.title: Noughts & Crosses
17:50.author: Malorie Blackman

17:51.quote: The sun was red and the shadows were long and dark. It wanted but nine minutes to six, and within the hotel the disgraceful National Swill was in full flow, men pouring as much liquor down their throats as possible before the fatal hour of drought arrived.
17:51.title: Venom House
17:51.author: Arthur Upfield

17:52.quote: It was eight minutes to six o'clock. 'I must get him,' he told the telephone girl for the dozenth time. 'Sorry - no one will answer,' she said wearily.
17:52.title: Jimmy Kirkland and the Plot for a Pennant
17:52.author: Hugh S. Fullerton

17:53.quote:
'That boy will be spoiled, as sure as I go on springs; he's made such a lot of. Have you been regulated?'
'I should think I have!' exclaimed I, in indignant recollection of my education. 'All right; keep your temper. What time are you?'
'Seven minutes to six.'
17:53.title: The Adventures of a Three-Guinea Watch
17:53.author: Talbot Baines Reed

17:54.quote: 'And yet,' Edward Henry Machin reflected as at six minutes to six he approached his own dwelling at the top of Bleakridge, 'and yet - I don't feel so jolly after all!'
17:54.title: The Old Adam: A Story of Adventure
17:54.author: Arnold Bennett

17:55.quote: 'I think we'll be lucky if we get away before they arrive.' At five minutes to six I was in position at the end of the strip with my engine running, ready for take-off.
17:55.title: Going Solo
17:55.author: Roald Dahl

17:55.quote: The clock over his head said five minutes to six. Shifting his glance from the clock, Jon became aware of a squat black robot waving to attract his attention.
17:55.title: The Velvet Glove
17:55.author: Harry Harrison

17:55.quote: When he reached the pier gates at five minutes to six, they were closed, and the obscure vista of the pier as deserted as some northern pier in mid-winter.
17:55.title: Clayhanger
17:55.author: Arnold Bennett

17:56.quote: Now, sir, the hand wants four minutes to six. If the hour strike before you call yourself a wanton, gratuitous calumniator, I 'll flog you round the room.'
17:56.title: The Knight Of Gwynne
17:56.author: Charles James Lever

17:57.quote: He would make his way by charm, the charm of voice, of jewel-like language, above all by the intellectual charm of new, moving, luminous ideas. At three minutes to six the doctor closed the book and waited.
17:57.title: The Drunkard
17:57.author: Guy Thorne

17:57.quote: Hugo Carmody had done this, with the result that by three minutes to six he was feeling as if he had been marooned among roses since the beginning of the summer.
17:57.title: Summer Lightning
17:57.author: P. G. Wodehouse

17:58.quote: 'Uh – yeah. Exactly. And what's the time now?' Then I notice the windows: they're shiny dark. 17:58, insists my watch. It can't be. It's only just gone four. I peer around my tormentor's belly to find Immaculée Constantin, but she's gone.
17:58.title: Bone Clocks
17:58.author: David Mitchell

17:59.quote: When he arrived it was nearly six o'clock, and the sun was setting full and warm, and the red light streamed in through the window and gave more colour to the pale cheeks.
17:59.title: Dracula
17:59.author: Bram Stoker

17:59.quote: It was nearly six o'clock in the evening, and the absurd bell in the six-foot tin steeple of the church went clank-clank, clank- clank! as old Mattu pulled the rope within.'
17:59.title: Burmese Days
17:59.author: George Orwell

18:00.quote: Although it was only six o'clock, the night was already dark. The fog, made thicker by its proximity to the Seine, blurred every detail with its ragged veils, punctured at various distances by the reddish glow of lanterns and bars of light escaping from illuminated windows.
18:00.title: Le Club des Hachichins
18:00.author: Théophile Gautier

18:00.quote: When he arrived it was nearly six o'clock, and the sun was setting full and warm, and the red light streamed in through the window and gave more colour to the pale cheeks.
18:00.title: Dracula
18:00.author: Bram Stoker

18:00.quote:
King Richard: What is o'clock?
Catesby: It is six o'clock, full supper time.
King Richard: I will not sup tonight. Give me some ink and paper.
18:00.title: Richard III
18:00.author: William Shakespeare

18:00.quote: The newspaper snaked through the door and there was suddenly a six o'clock feeling in the house
18:00.title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
18:00.author: Muriel Spark

18:01.quote: David had followed me at one minute past six. Then three others had managed to get away, making it five altogether. But as the sixth Hurricane was gathering speed for lift-off, a swarm of Messerschmitts had come swooping in over the olive trees.
18:01.title: Going Solo
18:01.author: Roald Dahl

18:02.quote: At two minutes past six o'clock, he stood outside of the coffee house as instructed, to the right of the entrance, and waited to be contacted. Over the noise of the street traffic and the guitar, he heard a soft tuneless jangling-tinkling.
18:02.title: Sole Survivor
18:02.author: Dean Koontz

18:03.quote: Above it all rose the Houses of Parliament, with the hands of the clock stopped at three minutes past six. It was difficult to believe that all that meant nothing any more, that now it was just a pretentious confection that could decay in peace.
18:03.title: The Day of the Triffids
18:03.author: John Wyndham

18:04.quote:
'We will make record of it, my Rosannah; every year, as this dear hour chimes from the clock, we will celebrate it with thanksgivings, all the years of our life.'
'We will, we will, Alonzo!'
'Four minutes after six, in the evening, my Rosannah...'
18:04.title: The Loves of Alonzo Fitz Clarence and Rosannah Ethelton
18:04.author: Mark Twain

18:05.quote: At about five past six Piers came in carrying an evening paper and a few books.
18:05.title: A Glass of Blessings
18:05.author: Barbara Pym

18:06.quote: 'According to the Book of Revelation, the great beast's number is 666. So that exact time would be six minutes past six on the sixth of the sixth, sixty-six. Double whammy.
18:06.title: A Dog Called Demolition
18:06.author: Robert Rankin

18:07.quote: The minutes ticked by. The silence in the room roared in their ears. At seven minutes past six, Charles's phone rang.
18:07.title: The Jury
18:07.author: Fern Michaels

18:08.quote: 6:08 p.m. The code-word 'Valkyrie' reached von Seydlitz Gabler's headquarters
18:08.title: The Night of the Generals
18:08.author: Hans Hellmut Kirst

18:09.quote: Here I am, at nine minutes past six on a drizzly Saturday evening, with every intention of writing a book in one sitting.
18:09.title: Life... With No Breaks
18:09.author: Nick Spalding

18:10.quote:
'Cut the city's communications,' I said. 'Excuse me?' came the startled response. 'Do it, and call me back when it's done—wait, Lewis! Are you there?'
'Yes, Rook Thomas.'
'How dark is it there now?' I asked. 'What?'
'How dark is it there? It's ten past six in the evening here, and it's dark.
18:10.title: The Rook
18:10.author: Daniel O'Malley

18:11.quote: The first explosion occurred at eleven minutes past six. The chart-house and part of the bridge were blown to pieces. Three dull, splintering crashes ensued in rapid succession, proving beyond question that the bombs were set to automatically explode at a given time.
18:11.title: West Wind Drift
18:11.author: George Barr McCutcheon

18:12.quote: He appeared to be haggling with the vendor about the amount of extra sauerkraut. Where was Harlequin? The clock on the tower of the Old Town Hall showed twelve minutes past six.
18:12.title: The Golem's Eye
18:12.author: Jonathan Stroud

18:13.quote: I take out my watch and it marks thirteen minutes past six. As I have already mentioned, I waited for the door of my prison to open, thoroughly resolved not to fall asleep again, but to spring upon the first person who entered and force him to answer my questions.
18:13.title: Facing the Flag
18:13.author: Jules Verne

18:14.quote: He has been for some years a victim to cataleptic attacks, on which, as is well known, Dr. Trevelyan is an authority. He proposes to call at about quarter past six to-morrow evening, if Dr. Trevelyan will make it convenient to be at home.'
18:14.title: The Resident Patient
18:14.author: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

18:15.quote: Quarter past six,' said Tony. 'He's bound to have told her by now.'
18:15.title: A Handful of Dust
18:15.author: Evelyn Waugh

18:16.quote: The clock above the door said sixteen minutes past six. The pictures showed the door opening and a rug coming in—just the rug, flat, held up perpendicular, hanging straight down. Of course there was someone behind it.
18:16.title: If Death Ever Slept
18:16.author: Rex Stout

18:17.quote: The printed front of the card read Bernis Sand. On the back was written in pencil: your westbound tram stop at 18:17. There was no date or signature. At 18:17 Adele stepped out of the townhouse with Tovera at her side.
18:17.title: The Way to Glory
18:17.author: David Drake

18:18.quote: It was at 18:18 when a sixty-one-year-old Tibetan woman telephoned for a fire engine to put out a burning car.
18:18.title: My Name is Not Jacob Ramsay
18:18.author: Ben Trebilcook

18:19.quote: I abort a groan. Gravesend's a black cloud. Vinny and Stella and Mam are in it. Are it. My watch says 18:19 and the Captain Marlow'll be cheerful and chattery as the evening regulars drift in.
18:19.title: The Bone Clocks
18:19.author: David Mitchell

18:20.quote: By the time Elliot's mother arrived at twenty past six, Mrs Sen always made sure all evidence of her chopping was disposed of.
18:20.title: Interpreter of Maladies
18:20.author: Jhumpa Lahiri

18:21.quote:
6.21 PM
Miss Pettigrew found herself wafted into the passage. She was past remonstrance now, past bewilderment, surprise, expostulation. Her eyes shone. Her face glowed. Her spirits soared. Everything was happening too quickly. She couldn't keep up with things, but, by golly, she could enjoy them.
18:21.title: Miss Pettigrew Lives for a Day
18:21.author: Winifred Watson

18:22.quote: Clock overturned when he fell forward. That'll give us the time of the crime. Twenty-two minutes past six.
18:22.title: The Murder at the Vicarage
18:22.author: Agatha Christie

18:23.quote: They surrounded it with excitement ... a picnic lunch in the kitchen, a double bolt drawn across the front door just in case the impossible happened and Father came home from the bank before twenty-three minutes past six in the evening.
18:23.title: Light A Penny Candle
18:23.author: Maeve Binchy

18:24.quote: We were cleared by the tower at 18:24 and got airborne seven minutes after the scheduled departure time. The evening sky was almost clear, with a scattering of cumulus along the southern horizon and the windsocks hanging limp.
18:24.title: Quiller's Run
18:24.author: Adam Hall

18:25.quote: I have this moment, while writing, had a wire from Jonathan saying that he leaves by the 6.25 tonight from Launceston and will be here at 10.18, so that I shall have no fear tonight.
18:25.title: Dracula
18:25.author: Bram Stoker

18:26.quote: I checked the time on my mobile phone – 18.26 p.m. 'Do you want to a grab a pizza?' Cara asked as she walked in twenty minutes later. I looked up at her. She shook her head smiling, questioning.
18:26.title: Play With Me
18:26.author: Ananth

18:27.quote: It's as much as your job is worth if a riot detail isn't down here in two minutes. Two minutes, I said. It is now precisely twenty-seven minutes past six o'clock.
18:27.title: Here Comes Civilization
18:27.author: William Tenn

18:27.quote: The pilot will carry a one-frequency low-power transmitter activated by the change in magnetic field on leaving the ship. The hopper will remain at five hundred feet until this signal is received. It will then pick up the pilot, check ground appearances, and rendezvous with this ship at two hundred miles up at 18.27 hours.
18:27.title: The Lost Kafoozalum
18:27.author: Pauline Ashwell

18:28.quote: Suit yourself, said Remo, turning on the TV. It was twenty-eight minutes past six. 'And, speaking of that barracuda, isn't she way overdue? Like into her ten or eleventh month?'
18:28.title: Terminal Transmission
18:28.author: Warren Murphy & Richard Sapir

18:29.quote: Lieutenant Hershel Mote could not keep the note of near hysteria out of his voice when Wesley returned his telephone call at twenty-nine minutes past six p. m. 'You're where?' Wesley asked him again on the speaker phone.
18:29.title: The Body Farm
18:29.author: Patricia Cornwell

18:30.quote: At five o'clock the two ladies retired to dress, and at half past six Elizabeth was summoned to dinner.
18:30.title: Pride and Prejudice
18:30.author: Jane Austen

18:30.quote: It is around half past six in the evening. Dusk is gathering in the living room, an early dusk due to the fog which has rolled in from the Sound and is like a white curtain drawn down outside the windows.
18:30.title: Long Day's Journey Into Night
18:30.author: Eugene O'Neill

18:31.quote: I had been delayed at a case and it was a little after half past six when I found myself at Baker Street once more.
18:31.title: The Adventure of The Blue Carbuncle
18:31.author: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle

18:32.quote: It stood on a low bookcase; its dial, white against black marble, swam out at him. And the hands stood at only twenty-eight minutes to seven.
18:32.title: Scandal at High Chimneys
18:32.author: John Dickson Carr

18:33.quote: Every evening, Michel took the train home, changed at Esbly and usually arrived in Crécy on the 6.33pm train where Annabelle would be waiting at the station
18:33.title: Atomised
18:33.author: Michel Houellebecq

18:34.quote: I knew I had to be on the 18.34 train in order to make it back to London in time for my final appointment of the day. As I sat there, though, I had time to think about my next joinee visit.
18:34.title: Join Me
18:34.author: Danny Wallace

18:35.quote: Matheson used the phone on the wall to make a 999 call and the police arrived on the scene at twenty-five minutes to seven. The body was removed to the mortuary and the room was searched, photographed and dusted for fingerprints.
18:35.title: Rumpole on Trial
18:35.author: John Mortimer

18:36.quote: Putt had picked up his bureau car at the Wickstown airport at 18.36 hours and driven straight through to the guard gate with no more trouble than a broken right rear spring and dented right fender.
18:36.title: The Search for the Dice Man
18:36.author: Luke Rhinehart

18:37.quote: Come to the 'Gray's-Inn Coffee-House,' Pinto said, 'and I will tell you how the notch came to the ax.' And we walked down Holborn at about thirty-seven minutes past six o'clock.
18:37.title: The Notch on the Ax
18:37.author: William Makepeace Thackeray

18:38.quote: He took the same route every day, spoke to the same security guard for the same amount of time, took the same lift to the same floor, hung his same coat in the same place and sat at the same desk until lunchtime, when he bought the same sandwich (ham and pickle on brown) from the same sandwich shop. At half past five every day he did the whole journey in reverse, reaching home at between 18:24 on a good day and 18:38 on a bad one.
18:38.title: The Case of the Wayward Professor
18:38.author: Gareth P. Jones

18:39.quote: At twenty-one minutes to seven, Yuri will sit down at your table, or maybe at the one next to it. That will obviously depend on how busy the cafe is.
18:39.title: Manhunt
18:39.author: James Barrington

18:40.quote: Having to change 'buses, I allowed plenty of time — in fact, too much; for we arrived at twenty minutes to seven, and Franching, so the servant said, had only just gone up to dress.
18:40.title: Diary of a Nobody
18:40.author: George & Weedon Grossmith

18:40.quote:
Amy: What's that? I thought I saw someone pass the window. What time is it?
Charles: Nearly twenty to seven.
18:40.title: The Family Reunion
18:40.author: T. S. Eliot

18:41.quote: He made it to Grand Central well in advance. Stillman's train was not due to arrive until six forty-one, but Quinn wanted time to study the geography of the place, to make sure that Stillman would not be able to slip away from him.
18:41.title: The New York Trilogy
18:41.author: Paul Auster

18:42.quote: Lo and behold, at 18:42 a young man entered the store, made a beeline for the magazine rack, and then fell over and started shaking like a leaf.
18:42.title: Whiskey Sour
18:42.author: J. A. Konrath

18:43.quote: Bev was no clock-watcher, but by 18.43 she'd had enough. A stack of papers at her left elbow represented eliminations: calls put in, checks made, follow-ups done and dusted. At the right lay further actions.
18:43.title: Bad Press
18:43.author: Maureen Carter

18:44.quote: 'As nearly as I can make out, he telephoned for the doctor about quarter before seven. He must have expired shortly after.'
18:44.title: The Luminous Face
18:44.author: Carolyn Wells

18:45.quote: It was a quarter to seven when I let myself into the office and clicked the light on and picked a piece of paper off the floor. It was a notice from the Green Feather Messenger Service ...
18:45.title: The High Window
18:45.author: Raymond Chandler

18:46.quote: When I heard the thump overhead, I did not bother to look up. I knew it was fourteen minutes to seven.
18:46.title: Tiffany Street
18:46.author: Jerome Weidman

18:47.quote: 'Radio contact was lost hours ago, and a distress signal was received at 18:47 hours from Captain Sheppard,' General Charles Braxton explained.
18:47.title: The Outer Rim
18:47.author: Alexis Martin

18:48.quote: We were soon dashing quickly along the streets. I did not know Liverpool well, and consequently could not exactly tell where the man was going. When I got into the hansom it wanted twelve minutes to seven o'clock; these minutes were quickly flying, and still no station.
18:48.title: A Master of Mysteries
18:48.author: Robert Eustace

18:49.quote: 6:49 p.m. Lieutenant-General Tanz escorted by a motorized unit, drove to Corps headquarters
18:49.title: The Night of the Generals
18:49.author: Hans Hellmut Kirst

18:50.quote: It was time to go see the Lady. When we arrived at her house at ten minutes before seven o'clock, Damaronde answered the door.
18:50.title: Boy's Life
18:50.author: Robert R. McCammon

18:51.quote: The square of light in the kitchen doorway had faded to thin purple; his watch said 6:51.
18:51.title: Salem's Lot
18:51.author: Stephen King

18:52.quote: From across the railway lines she observed Kevin, ticket in hand, come onto the London-bound platform and catch the 18.52 to Fenchurch Street.
18:52.title: Unsafe Deposit
18:52.author: J. E. Kellenberger

18:53.quote: He arrived at the playground at seven minutes to seven. It was dark out already, and the mercury-vapor lamp shone its even, artificial illumination into every nook and cranny of the tiny park.
18:53.title: 1Q84: Book 3
18:53.author: Haruki Murakami

18:53.quote: I arranged my papers, made a brief review of matters which would come before me later, and with muddy boots and heavy heart I arrived at the station at seven minutes to seven and took the slow train for London. When I told the story of my adventures at dinner a soldier friend remarked: 'You've been more than a little lucky in getting away at all. I was very unlucky when I applied—' But his story was a long one, and I have forgotten it.
18:53.title: The Amateur Army
18:53.author: Patrick MacGill

18:54.quote: 'I saw him do it. It was only – only...' He looked at his watch. The hands were stuck in place, stopped for ever at six minutes to seven.
18:54.title: Abhorsen
18:54.author: Garth Nix

18:55.quote: ... You had no reason to think the times important. Indeed how suspicious it would be if you had been completely accurate. 'Haven't I been?' Not quite. It was five to seven that you talked to Wilkins. 'Another ten minutes.'
18:55.title: The Quiet American
18:55.author: Graham Greene

18:56.quote: Then it was 6.56. A black Rover - a Rover 90, registration PYX 520 - turned into the street that ran down the left-hand side of The Bunker. It parked. The door on the driver's side opened. A man got out.
18:56.title: Dreams of Leaving
18:56.author: Rupert Thomson

18:57.quote: Harry clocked on at three minutes to seven. 'The truck?' I asked. 'Where we talked about.'
18:57.title: The Green Mile
18:57.author: Stephen King

18:58.quote:
'Walk fast,' says Perry, 'it's two minutes to seven, and I got to be home by—'
'Oh, shut up,' says I. 'I had an appointment as chief performer at an inquest at seven, and I'm not kicking about not keeping it.'
18:58.title: Roads of Destiny
18:58.author: O. Henry

18:59.quote: At one minute to seven exactly Zen walked through to the inner hallway where the phone was and dialled the number Tania had given him. A woman answered.
18:59.title: Vendetta
18:59.author: Michael Dibdin

18:59.quote: It was near on seven o'clock when I got to Mr. and Mrs. Fleming's house on 6th Street, where I was renting a room. It was late September, and though there was some sun left, I didn't want to visit a dead man's place with night coming on.
18:59.title: All Aunt Hagar's Children
18:59.author: Edward P. Jones

19:00.quote: It was seven o'clock and by this time she was not very far from Raveloe, but she was not familiar enough with those monotonous lanes to know how near she was to her journey's end. She needed comfort, and she knew but one comforter - the familiar demon in her bosom; but she hesitated a moment, after drawing out the black remnant, before she raised it to her lips.
19:00.title: Silas Marner
19:00.author: George Eliot

19:00.quote: Twas about seven o'clock at night, And the wind it blew with all its might, And the rain came pouring down, And the dark clouds seem'd to frown,
19:00.title: The Tay Bridge Disaster
19:00.author: William McGonagall

19:00.quote: About seven o'clock in the evening she had died, and her frantic husband had made a frightful scene in his efforts to kill West, whom he wildly blamed for not saving her life. Friends had held him when he drew a stiletto, but West departed amidst his inhuman shrieks, curses, and oaths of vengeance.
19:00.title: Herbert West - Reanimator
19:00.author: H. P. Lovecraft

19:00.quote: The town clock struck seven. The echoes of the great chime wandered in the unlit halls of the library. An autumn leaf, very crisp, fell somewhere in the dark. But it was only the page of a book, turning.
19:00.title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
19:00.author: Ray Bradbury

19:01.quote: The gates of the cemetery were closed at sundown; sundown took place that night at one minute past seven, and the distance into town is considerable.
19:01.title: The Filigree Ball
19:01.author: Anna Katharine Green

19:02.quote: When Teddy Carella walked into the squad room at two minutes past seven, Peter Byrnes thought he would have a heart attack.
19:02.title: Killer's Wedge
19:02.author: Ed McBain

19:03.quote: Tengo glanced at his watch. The hands showed three past seven, ticking away the time ever more accurately. Still no Aomame. Tengo spent several minutes gazing at the hands of his watch as if they were something extraordinary.
19:03.title: 1Q84: Book 3
19:03.author: Haruki Murakami

19:03.quote: Being a man of means, he could have moved in better circles; he might have associated with his father's friends, and he did so, twice a year; but he was of too despotic a nature to get on with them. It was three minutes past seven and still the guests had not arrived. Falk began to show signs of impatience.
19:03.title: The Red Room
19:03.author: August Strindberg

19:04.quote: Sunday evening at almost the same hour (to be precise, at about 7:04 p.m.) she rings the front door bell at the home of Walter Moeding, Crime Commissioner, who is at that moment engaged, for professional rather than private reasons, in disguising himself as a sheikh.
19:04.title: The Lost Honour of Katharina Blum
19:04.author: Heinrich Böll

19:04.quote: It was now four minutes past seven, meaning they had a little under two hours before the curtain went up at the Alhambra.
19:04.title: Moriarty
19:04.author: John Gardner

19:04.quote: At four minutes past seven this evening I made contact with Redruth in the Café Balzac.
19:04.title: The Secret Families
19:04.author: John Gardner

19:05.quote: I found the table spread, and the supper ready. I shut up Zenobia in the room where the ladies were to make their toilette, and at five minutes past seven the joyous company arrived.
19:05.title: The Complete Memoirs of Jacques Casanova de Seingalt
19:05.author: Giacomo Casanova

19:06.quote: The time on that clock was six minutes after seven p.m. That doesn't conclusively prove that Sam Dillon was murdered at seven-oh-six p.m., but all other evidence would certainly corroborate that point.”
19:06.title: In the Company of Liars
19:06.author: David Ellis

19:07.quote: She fell into unconsciousness and so remained two days and five hours, until Tuesday evening at seven minutes past seven, when the release came. She was twenty-four years and five months old. On the 23d, her mother and her sisters saw her laid to rest—she that had been our wonder and our worship.
19:07.title: Chapters from My Autobiography
19:07.author: Mark Twain

19:08.quote: It was eight minutes past seven and still no girl. I waited impatiently. I watched another crowd surge through the barriers and move quickly down the steps. My eyes were alert for the faintest recognition.
19:08.title: The Girl from East Berlin
19:08.author: James Furner

19:09.quote: Wallander was lucky and managed to catch a taxi right outside. When he got to Rosengard it was nine minutes past seven. He hoped that Mona was running late. But when he read the note he had posted on the door he realised that this was not the case.
19:09.title: The Pyramid
19:09.author: Henning Mankell

19:10.quote: At seven-ten not a soul had come; somewhat acrimoniously, the family discussed the advantages and disadvantages of tardiness
19:10.title: The Elderly Lady
19:10.author: Jorge Luis Borges

19:10.quote: He had already got to the point where, by rocking more strongly, he maintained his equilibrium with difficulty, and very soon he would finally have to make a final decision, for in five minutes it would be a quarter past seven.
19:10.title: Metamorphosis
19:10.author: Franz Kafka

19:11.quote: At eleven past seven, Lucas's car could be heard making its way to the end of my gravelly road. Mena had her hand hanging out of her window, letting it fly through the wind, as they pulled up and right beside where I stood.
19:11.title: Where Things Come Back
19:11.author: John Corey Whaley

19:12.quote: Salander fast-forwarded through the pictures from the other PC, which photographed the gate and the road outside the front door. She could see who had gone past during the day. At 19:12 Nilsson came home.
19:12.title: The Girl With The Dragon Tattoo
19:12.author: Stieg Larsson

19:13.quote: 'Look!' He held it up with the face toward us. The hands stood at thirteen minutes past seven. 'The hour and the moment when it struck the floor,' he declared. 'And consequently the hour and the moment when Mrs. Jeffrey fell,' finished Durbin. Miss Tuttle said nothing, only gasped. 'Valuable evidence,' quoth the captain, putting the watch in his pocket.
19:13.title: The Filigree Ball
19:13.author: Anna Katharine Green

19:13.quote: The train arrived at Kizil Arvat, two hundred and forty-two versts from the Caspian, at thirteen minutes past seven in the evening instead of seven o'clock. This slight delay provoked thirteen objurgations from the baron, one for each minute.
19:13.title: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent
19:13.author: Jules Verne

19:14.quote: If he'd lived in New York and worked in an office, he might have thrived as the typical, over-martini'd, cheating husband, leaving every night on the 7:14 to White Plains, a smudge of lipstick high on his neck, and a tide of lies to see him through to the next day.
19:14.title: The Life and Opinions of Maf the Dog, and of his friend Marilyn Monroe
19:14.author: Andrew O'Hagan

19:15.quote: Nick had a large wild plan of his own for the night, but for now he let Leo take charge: they were going to go back to Notting Hill and catch the seven fifteen screening of Scarface at the Gate.
19:15.title: The Line of Beauty
19:15.author: Alan Hollinghurst

19:16.quote: To-day is Monday, the fifth of August, and the sun sets in sixteen minutes past seven. It will be dark when we get to headquarters.
19:16.title: The Boy Patrol on Guard
19:16.author: Edward Sylvester Ellis

19:17.quote: Colonel Putnis knocked on his door at 7.17 p.m. The car was waiting in front of the hotel, and they drove through the dark streets to police headquarters. It had grown much colder during the evening, and the city was almost deserted.
19:17.title: The Dogs of Riga
19:17.author: Henning Mankell

19:18.quote: Gretchen looked at her watch 'It's now exactly eighteen minutes past seven, according to Mr Cartier.' It was still raining outside; but a taxi drove up and a couple got out and the doorman protected Gretchen with a big umbrella as she ran for the cab.
19:18.title: Rich Man, Poor Man
19:18.author: Irwin Shaw

19:19.quote: The cabbie had bent the speed limit, and Sigrid, who normally hated fast driving, gratefully added a little extra to her tip as he let her out in Sussex Square. It was only nineteen minutes past seven. Fashionably late, she told herself and hurried up the brick walk.
19:19.title: Corpus Christmas
19:19.author: Margaret Maron

19:20.quote: The pause, we finally concluded, was to allow the other important people to catch up, those who had arrived at 7:10 waiting for those who had arrived at 7:20.
19:20.title: Parkinson's Law or the Pursuit of Progress
19:20.author: C. Northcote Parkinson

19:21.quote: Gripping her gym bag in her right hand, Aomame, like Buzzcut, was waiting for something to happen. The clock display changed to 7:21
19:21.title: 1Q84
19:21.author: Haruki Murakami

19:22.quote: Gripping her gym bag in her right hand, Aomame, like Buzzcut, was waiting for something to happen. The clock display changed to 7:21, then 7:22
19:22.title: 1Q84
19:22.author: Haruki Murakami

19:23.quote:
'What time did the last train arrive from Liverpool?' asked Thomas Flanagan. 'At twenty-three minutes past seven,' replied Gauthier Ralph; 'and the next does not arrive till ten minutes after twelve.'
'Well, gentlemen,' resumed Andrew Stuart, 'if Phileas Fogg had come in the 7:23 train, he would have got here by this time.
19:23.title: Around the World in Eighty Days
19:23.author: Jules Verne

19:24.quote: From the buildings opposite, a recorded announcement crackled into life, echoing out under the roof awning. 'Platform one for the 19.24 South West Trains service to Alton, calling at Alton only.' The voice had a disjointed quality – sentences and station names, assembled into an announcement by a computer.
19:24.title: Knife Edge
19:24.author: Fergus McNeill

19:25.quote: He picked up his hat and coat and Clarice said hello to him and he said hello and looked at the clock and it was almost twenty-five after seven.
19:25.title: The Evening's at Seven
19:25.author: James Thurber

19:26.quote: It was now twenty-six minutes past seven, desperately close to the time limit. The queue of people ten minutes before had dwindled to nothing. No-one else wanted to cut it that fine and the last was paying up now.
19:26.title: 7th Sense
19:26.author: John McLaren

19:26.quote: Newman pointed up at the hotel. Under the eaves shards of ice a foot long projected downwards. A palisade of icicles. Inverted. The station was little more than a one-storey hut, an isolated building with no one about. The dashboard clock registered 19.26 hours.
19:26.title: Terminal
19:26.author: Colin Forbes

19:27.quote: He may as well give it a go, at least once. If it was as bad as he was anticipating, he need never return. He turned into the college car park at twenty-seven minutes past seven precisely.
19:27.title: Life Drawing for Beginners
19:27.author: Roisin Meaney

19:28.quote: At twenty-eight minutes past seven it struck twelve again. In a rage Leslie raised her head and shouted, 'Go away, Alison, this is my study now!' and then was glad of the soundproofing.
19:28.title: The Inheritor
19:28.author: Marion Zimmer Bradley

19:29.quote: Nicholas and Uri were setting the places when Edward Kitchener and Rosette Harding-Clarke came in at twenty-nine minutes past seven.
19:29.title: A Quantum Murder
19:29.author: Peter F. Hamilton

19:30.quote: 'From the timings given by the Perimeter,' Horyse said, 'this fog - and whatever's in it - is moving southwards at around twenty miles an hour. As the crow flies, it'll reach us around half past seven. Dark, with the moonrise yet to come.'
19:30.title: Sabriel
19:30.author: Garth Nix

19:30.quote: The clock showed half-past seven. This was the twilight time. He would be there now. I pictured him in his old navy-blue sweater and peaked cap, walking soft-footed up the track towards the wood.
19:30.title: Danny the Champion of the World
19:30.author: Roald Dahl

19:30.quote: But now he was close - here was the house, here were the gates. Somewhere a clock beat a single chime. 'What, is it really half-past seven? That's impossible, it must be fast!'
19:30.title: Crime and Punishment
19:30.author: Fyodor Dostoyevsky

19:31.quote: I was invited to the Sugiyama residence, outside Kanazawa in the Ishikawa prefecture of Chubu, and arrived at 19:31 UT. The Sugiyama bash'house is a compact town house, three stories, pressed tightly on both sides by similar houses.
19:31.title: Too Like the Lightning
19:31.author: Ada Palmer

19:32.quote: My fingers play with the lid of the gin but I stop myself and open a bottle of red instead. The little green numbers read 19.32 when I hear the scrunch of footsteps on the drive.
19:32.title: The Cheesemaker's House
19:32.author: Jane Cable

19:33.quote: At thirty-three minutes past seven he stood on the platform of the station at Southampton—a clear hour before the train containing Owen could possibly arrive. Making a few inquiries here, but too impatient to pursue his investigation carefully and inductively, he went into the town.
19:33.title: Desperate Remedies
19:33.author: Thomas Hardy

19:34.quote: I checked my watch at 19:34 and thought that would be unfortunate, wouldn't it, if Pepperidge finally called me and heard only the engaged tone while Al was asking about his girlfriend's health or his aunt's or whoever the hell it was on the other end, my chest's easier but there's still this cough, and the doctor says I do not care what the doctor says, just get off the line.
19:34.title: Quiller's Run
19:34.author: Adam Hall

19:35.quote: It was twenty-five minutes to eight; his guests would be coming to dine at eight o'clock and he had not begun to dress.
19:35.title: The Reason Why
19:35.author: Elinor Glyn
